change_reason: WI-3381 / bridge gtkb-gov-requirements-collection-hook-tag-cleanup: v5 tag-only supersession removing stale abandoned-design tags; owner approved tag-only supersession in chat on 2026-06-23.
explicit_change_request: AUQ CHAT-2026-06-23-WI-3381-GOV-V5-TAG-ONLY-SUPERSESSION: Approve WI-3381 GOV v5 tag-only supersession
full_content:
A UserPromptSubmit hook MUST detect canonical specification-language triggers via fixed regex patterns and direct the AI agent to use AskUserQuestion to confirm any specification creation triggered by the hook.

AMENDMENT NOTE (v2 supersedes v1):
v1 mandated a lightweight LLM classifier (Haiku-class) with 4-label classification, retrieval-augmented options, and 3-option clarification flow. Per S332 owner directive ('We will not add an API key for parallel API usage. That incurs an unacceptable additional cost.'), the LLM-classifier requirement is removed in v2. v2 retains the AUQ-only-spec-creation invariant, which the owner identified as the more important property: 'It is more important that no requirements specifications are created without my explicit choice from an AskUserQuestion.'

DETECTION (binding):
- The hook MUST run on every owner message submitted via UserPromptSubmit.
- The hook MUST detect canonical specification-language triggers using a fixed set of regex patterns.
- The trigger set MUST be small and intuitive enough for the owner to remember and use as a contract: the owner uses explicit canonical phrasings to invoke specification work.
- Anti-patterns (commands, questions, affirmations) and a minimum prompt length filter MUST suppress false positives.
- The hook MUST NOT make external API calls (no LLM, no retrieval-augmented options).

AUQ-ONLY SPEC CREATION INVARIANT (binding):
When the hook fires, the AI agent MUST NOT create or promote any formal artifact (SPEC, REQ, GOV, ADR, DCL, PB, DELIB) without first issuing an AskUserQuestion to confirm the artifact's existence, scope, and approval. The agent MUST surface the candidate to the owner immediately with classification, interpretation, and the artifact that would be created if approved, then wait for the AskUserQuestion answer before any KB mutation. This invariant aligns with GOV-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY-001's positive-path surfacing requirement.

OUTPUT (binding):
- The hook MUST emit either:
  - {"systemMessage": "<reminder text>"} when a canonical trigger is detected, OR
  - {} (empty JSON object) when no trigger is detected.
- The reminder text MUST contain the strings 'AskUserQuestion' and 'do NOT create' to make the AUQ-only invariant explicit and machine-checkable.
- The hook MUST exit 0 always; errors are written to stderr without blocking the user prompt.

IMPLEMENTATION CONTRACT:
Per DCL-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-CONTRACT-001 v2 (binding implementation contract). The doctor-invariant set verifies hook presence, tracked-settings registration, Codex-parity registration, and test presence.

RATIONALE:
The LLM-classifier design in v1 traded API cost and latency for richer classification across 5 forms (imperative, descriptive, structural, comparative, inferred). Owner cost analysis determined that trade is unfavorable: regex triggers + owner-explicit-language convention satisfies the substantive goal (no spec created without AUQ confirmation) without per-message API spend or 5-15s latency per UserPromptSubmit. The owner adopts the convention of using canonical trigger phrases when they want spec-creation behavior; the hook fires the AUQ reminder; no formal artifacts land without owner-visible AskUserQuestion approval.
